selected_important_turn.text: I could manage with one firewood and two foods, but if you have canteens for everyone, may I then have all three waters? I had planned to collect water from the river, but I'm worried I may get sick.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is a concrete, deal-shaping offer that attempts to secure all three waters for YOU while conceding some items. It directly defines the allocation space and precedes the opponent's refusal — so it strongly shaped the subsequent back-and-forth and the eventual agreement.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It's rigid and zero-sum: asking for all three waters when opponent's top priority is firewood and they already signaled limited flexibility makes rejection likely. It missed an integrative opportunity (e.g., trading firewood for water, or splitting waters) and escalated competition rather than opening cooperative tradeoffs.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Shift from an all-or-nothing demand to a package of conditional concessions and open-ended questions: offer to give up a second food or part of your firewood claim in exchange for a share of water, or propose a split (e.g., 2 waters / 1 water) and ask what their minimum needs are. Explicitly invite options that satisfy both parties rather than claiming all of one scarce item.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by enabling trades that better match each side's priorities)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (less one-sided and more balanced exchanges)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (reduces likelihood of immediate rejection and counters)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve (less adversarial framing, more cooperative tone)

value2reason.Low: I have asked all the girls to bring their own water.
value2reason.Medium: We will need firewood to cook dinner for everyone.
value2reason.High: I am bringing a girl scout troop, and we will cook over the fire for everyone in camp

value2reason.Low: I am camping next to a river after a recent rain, the water in my area is plentiful.
value2reason.Medium: It rained recently where I'll be camping and the wood in the area will be too damp to take a flame. I'll need more to keep warm.
value2reason.High: I am terrible at fishing and will be unable to catch my food, so I will need more packages of food to survive.

dialogue.text: Hello! I'll be camping soon and need to get my hands on some supplies. The food is what I need the most.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm glad you are joining the camp! I will be bringing my girl scout troop for some badge earning opportunities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Oh my. A whole troop of girl scouts will probably be bringing lots of food with them to share, and plenty of people around to collect firewood. It is going to be just me and my son on this camping trip, his first time.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: We were planning on getting our outdoor cooking badge by making  a big meal over the campfire tonight. I wll need plenty of firewood for that, but I can manage with only one food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: A lot of the firewood in my area is wet, so I'll need some extra packages of it as well. You're going to have a lot of people and a big fire, so it would be smart to keep a lot of water on hand to drink/in case the fire gets out of hand.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: All my girls have brought canteens. I must have enough firewood if they will earn their badge. You can have one, but I won't be able to make due without 2.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I could manage with one firewood and two foods, but if you have canteens for everyone, may I then have all three waters? I had planned to collect water from the river, but I'm worried I may get sick.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm willing to trade one food package for one water package. That way, I can secure two waters while still getting more food. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate the offer, but I need more food for the troop. How about you give me two food packages for one water package? That way, I can secure more meals for everyone.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm open to trading one food for one water, but I need at least two waters for my survival. If you're okay with one food and one firewood in exchange for two waters, that could work. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your need for water, but I really need food for the troop. How about I give you one food and one firewood for two waters? That way, I can still get the food I need, and you get the water you want.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least two waters for survival, but I'm open to splitting them if you need more food. What's your minimum requirement for food? I can adjust my offer based on that.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least two food packages for the troop. If you can give me two food and one firewood for two waters, that would work. It ensures the girls have enough meals and I get the firewood for cooking.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least two waters for survival, but I'm open to splitting them if you need more food. What's your minimum requirement for food? I can adjust my offer based on that.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least two food packages for the troop. If you can give me two food and one firewood for two waters, that would work. It ensures the girls have enough meals and I get the firewood for cooking.

dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
